Infections in certain parts of the body, like the prostate, bladder, or seminal vesicles, can cause a strong burning or itching feeling after ejaculation. - The prostate is a small gland in men that helps make semen. - The bladder is where urine is stored. - The seminal vesicles are small sacs that also help produce semen. If you notice these symptoms, it’s important to talk to a doctor. They can check for an infection and recommend the right treatment.

Example 94 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 94
Source Text
Clinical evaluation
Generated Simplification
A clinical evaluation is a process where a doctor or healthcare provider checks your health to understand your condition better. This may include: - Asking about your symptoms (how you feel and what problems you’re experiencing). - Reviewing your medical history (past illnesses, surgeries, or treatments). - Performing a physical exam (checking your body for signs of illness). Sometimes, the doctor may order tests, like blood work or imaging (such as X-rays), to get more information. This evaluation helps the doctor figure out what might be causing your symptoms and decide on the best next steps for your care. If you have questions or concerns, talk to your doctor during this process.
Reference Simplification
A doctor 's evaluation

Example 96 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 96
Source Text
Common warts (verrucae vulgaris) are caused by HPV types 1, 2, 4, and 7 and occasionally other types in immunosuppressed patients (eg, 75 to 77). They are usually asymptomatic but sometimes cause mild pain when they are located on a weight-bearing surface (eg, bottom of the feet).
Generated Simplification
Common warts are small, rough bumps on the skin caused by a virus called HPV (human papillomavirus). There are different types of HPV that can cause these warts, including types 1, 2, 4, and 7. Most of the time, common warts don’t hurt or cause any problems. However, if a wart is on a part of the body you put pressure on, like the bottom of your foot, it might cause mild pain. People with weaker immune systems may sometimes get warts from other types of HPV as well. If you’re concerned about warts or have questions, talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
Most warts are painless, but some hurt when touched. Warts on the feet can cause pain when standing or walking.

Example 101 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 101
Source Text
Dementia also occurs in patients with Parkinson disease, Huntington disease, progressive supranuclear palsy, Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease, Gerstmann-StrÃ¤ussler-Scheinker syndrome, other prion disorders, and neurosyphilis.
Generated Simplification
Some medical conditions can lead to dementia. Dementia is a condition that affects the brain, making it harder to think, remember, and carry out daily activities. Here are some conditions that may cause dementia: - Parkinson's disease: A disorder that affects movement and can also impact memory and thinking over time. - Huntington's disease: A genetic condition that causes changes in the brain, leading to problems with movement, thinking, and emotions. - Progressive supranuclear palsy: A rare brain disorder that affects balance, movement, and thinking. - Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease: A very rare condition caused by abnormal proteins in the brain, leading to rapid brain damage. - Gerstmann-Sträussler-Scheinker syndrome: A rare inherited condition that affects the brain and nervous system. - Other prion disorders: These are diseases caused by abnormal proteins that damage the brain. - Neurosyphilis: A late-stage complication of syphilis, an infection that can harm the brain and nervous system if not treated. If you or someone you know is experiencing memory problems or changes in thinking, it’s important to talk to a doctor. They can help find the cause and discuss treatment options.
Reference Simplification
Disorders that can cause dementia include the following: Parkinson disease (a common cause); Brain damage due to a head injury or certain tumors; Huntington disease; Prion diseases, such as Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (which causes a rapidly progressive dementia); Progressive supranuclear palsy; Neurosyphilis.
BERTScore
Precision
-0.098
Recall
0.408
F1
0.144

C2 2 Short background explanations are provided for each condition, such as describing how they affect the brain or body.
C3 2 The relationships between the conditions and dementia are made explicit.

S1 2 Long sentences are split into shorter, easier-to-process sentences.
S2 2 Active voice is used throughout the simplification, making the meaning clearer.

P2 2 The simplification makes options and consequences explicit by advising patients to talk to a doctor for diagnosis and treatment.
P3 2 Warnings and next steps are clearly stated, such as the importance of consulting a doctor for memory problems.

Example 106 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 106
Source Text
Ehrlichiosis and anaplasmosis may result in disseminated intravascular coagulation, multiorgan failure, seizures, and coma.
Generated Simplification
Ehrlichiosis and anaplasmosis are infections caused by bacteria. If these infections become severe, they can lead to serious health problems, including: - Blood clotting issues, which can cause bleeding in different parts of the body (this is called disseminated intravascular coagulation). - Failure of important organs, like the heart, kidneys, or liver, to work properly (this is called multiorgan failure). - Seizures, which are sudden, uncontrolled movements or changes in behavior caused by abnormal brain activity. - Coma, which is a deep state of unconsciousness where a person cannot wake up or respond. If you think you might have one of these infections or are experiencing symptoms, it’s important to talk to a doctor right away. Early treatment can help prevent these serious complications.
Reference Simplification
Ehrlichiosis and anaplasmosis may cause widespread blood clotting (disseminated intravascular coagulation), severe malfunction (failure) of several organs, seizures, and coma.
BERTScore
Precision
-0.01
Recall
0.571
F1
0.265

Source Text
Encephalitis causes restlessness, confusion, agitation, bizarre behavior, hallucinations, and insomnia.
Generated Simplification
Encephalitis is a condition that causes swelling in the brain. This swelling can lead to several symptoms, including: - Feeling very restless or unable to stay calm. - Being confused or having trouble thinking clearly. - Acting in an unusual or agitated way. - Seeing or hearing things that aren’t really there (hallucinations). - Having trouble sleeping or not being able to sleep at all (insomnia). If you or someone you know has these symptoms, it’s important to talk to a doctor right away.

Most people become restless, confused, and uncontrollably excited. Their behavior may be bizarre. They may hallucinate and have insomnia.

Example 108 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 108
Source Text
Endoscopy with biopsy
Generated Simplification
An endoscopy with biopsy is a medical procedure that helps doctors look inside your body and take a small sample of tissue for testing. Here’s how it works: - During an endoscopy, a thin, flexible tube with a tiny camera on the end is gently passed into your body. This allows the doctor to see inside areas like your stomach, esophagus (the tube that connects your mouth to your stomach), or intestines. - If the doctor sees anything unusual, they may take a small piece of tissue. This is called a biopsy. The tissue is sent to a lab to check for problems, like infections or signs of disease. The procedure is usually done to help find the cause of symptoms like stomach pain, trouble swallowing, or unexplained weight loss. If your doctor recommends an endoscopy with biopsy, they will explain how to prepare and what to expect. Be sure to ask any questions you have.
Reference Simplification
Endoscopy and biopsy
BERTScore
Precision
-0.26
Recall
0.255
F1
-0.014

Source Text
Erythema marginatum is a serpiginous, flat or slightly raised, nonscarring, and painless rash. The rash usually appears on the trunk and proximal extremities but not the face. It sometimes lasts < 1 day.
Generated Simplification
Erythema marginatum is a type of skin rash. Here’s what you need to know about it: - The rash has a wavy or snake-like shape. - It is flat or slightly raised but does not leave scars. - It does not cause pain. - The rash usually shows up on the chest, back, arms, or legs, but not on the face. - Sometimes, the rash goes away in less than a day. If you notice a rash like this, it’s a good idea to talk to a doctor to find out what might be causing it.
Reference Simplification
A flat, painless rash with a wavy edge (erythema marginatum) may appear as the other symptoms subside. It lasts for only a short time, sometimes less than a day.

Example 115 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 115
Source Text
For example, stacks of hoarded newspapers may fill the sink and cover the countertops and stove in the kitchen, preventing these areas from being used to prepare meals.
Generated Simplification
Sometimes, people may collect and keep too many items, like old newspapers. These newspapers can pile up and take over important spaces in the home, such as the kitchen. For example: - The sink might be filled with newspapers, making it hard to wash dishes. - The countertops and stove could be covered, so there’s no space to prepare or cook meals. This can make it difficult to use the kitchen for its normal purpose.
Reference Simplification
For example, stacks of hoarded newspapers may fill the sink and cover the countertops, stove, and floor in the kitchen, preventing the kitchen from being used to prepare meals.

Source Text
Immunodeficiency occurs when the immune system is less active than normal, resulting in recurring and life-threatening infections.
Generated Simplification
The immune system is your body’s defense against germs and infections. Immunodeficiency happens when the immune system is weaker than it should be. This makes it harder for your body to fight off infections. People with immunodeficiency may get infections more often, and these infections can sometimes be very serious.
Reference Simplification

Example 119 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 119
Source Text
By using the term adaptation for the evolutionary process, and adaptive trait for the bodily part or function ( the product ), one may distinguish the two different senses of the word. Adaptation is one of the two main processes that explain the observed diversity of species, such as the different species of Darwin 's finches.
Generated Simplification
Adaptation is a word that can mean two different things in science. Here's how they are different: - **Adaptation (the process):** This is the way living things change over a long time to survive better in their environment. For example, animals or plants may develop new features to help them live in their surroundings. - **Adaptive trait (the result):** This is the specific body part or ability that helps an animal or plant survive. For example, a bird might have a special beak shape that helps it eat certain foods. Adaptation is one of the main reasons why there are so many different types of animals and plants in the world. For example, Darwin's finches are birds that live on the Galápagos Islands. They have different beak shapes because they adapted to eat different kinds of food.
Reference Simplification
By using the term adaptation for the evolutionary process, and adaptive trait for the bodily part or function ( the product ), the two senses of the word may be distinguished.
BERTScore
Precision
0.034
Recall
0.22
F1
0.127

Example 121 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 121
Source Text
Genitourinary syndrome of menopause includes symptoms and signs due to estrogen deficiency such as Vulvovaginal atrophy; Urinary urgency; Dysuria; Frequent UTIs.
Generated Simplification
During menopause, some women experience a group of symptoms called genitourinary syndrome of menopause. These symptoms happen because the body makes less estrogen, a hormone that helps keep certain tissues healthy. Here’s what you might notice: - **Vaginal dryness or discomfort**: The tissues in and around the vagina can become thinner and less elastic, which may cause dryness or irritation. This is sometimes called vulvovaginal atrophy. - **Feeling like you need to urinate often or urgently**: You might feel a sudden, strong urge to go to the bathroom, even if your bladder isn’t very full. - **Pain or burning when urinating**: This is known as dysuria and can feel uncomfortable. - **Frequent urinary tract infections (UTIs)**: You may notice you’re getting UTIs more often than before. If you’re experiencing any of these symptoms, it’s a good idea to talk to your doctor. They can help explain your options for managing these changes and improving your comfort.
Reference Simplification
Genitourinary syndrome of menopause is a new, more accurate term used to refer to symptoms that affect the vagina and urinary tract and that are caused by menopause, such as vaginal dryness, pain during sexual intercourse, urinary urgency, and urinary tract infections.
BERTScore
Precision
-0.058
Recall
0.371
F1
0.149

Source Text
Sociobiology investigates social behaviors, such as mating patterns, territorial fights, pack hunting, and the hive society of social insects.
Generated Simplification
Sociobiology is the study of how animals behave in groups. It looks at things like: - How animals choose mates. - How they fight to protect their space. - How they work together to hunt for food. - How insects, like bees or ants, live and work together in groups, such as hives. It helps us understand why animals act the way they do in social situations.
Reference Simplification
Ethology investigates collective animal behavior, such as mating patterns, territorial fights, pack hunting, and the hive society of social insects.

If you think you might have been exposed to rabies, talk to a doctor right away.

Example 140 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 140
Source Text
Thalassemia can cause significant complications, including iron overload, bone deformities and cardiovascular illness.
Generated Simplification
Thalassemia is a blood condition that can lead to serious health problems. These may include: - Too much iron in the body, which can harm organs like the heart and liver. - Changes in the shape of bones, especially in the face and skull. - Heart problems, such as issues with blood flow or heart function. If you have thalassemia, it’s important to talk to your doctor about how to manage these risks.
Reference Simplification

Example 141 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 141
Source Text
Lyme disease has 3 stages: Early localized; Early disseminated; Late.
Generated Simplification
Lyme disease happens in three stages: 1. **Early localized stage**: This is the first stage. Symptoms usually show up soon after a tick bite. 2. **Early disseminated stage**: This is the second stage. The infection starts to spread to other parts of the body. 3. **Late stage**: This is the third stage. It happens if the disease is not treated earlier and can cause more serious problems. If you think you might have Lyme disease, talk to your doctor. Early treatment is important.
Reference Simplification
Lyme disease has three stages: Early localized; Early disseminated (widespread); Late.

Example 142 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 142
Source Text
The nervous system consists of a ventral nerve cord, with one ganglion in each segment, and an anterior nerve ring surrounding the pharynx.
Generated Simplification
The nervous system is like a communication network in the body. - It has a main cord, called the ventral nerve cord, that runs along the body. - This cord has small clusters of nerve cells, called ganglia, in each body segment. These ganglia help control movement and other functions in that segment. - At the front of the body, near the throat (pharynx), there is a nerve ring. This ring helps connect the nervous system in the head to the rest of the body. This system works together to send messages and control the body’s actions.
Reference Simplification
It consists of a ventral nerve cord, with one ganglion in each segment, and an anterior nerve ring surrounding the pharynx.

If you notice any unusual symptoms in these areas, talk to your doctor.

Example 144 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 144
Source Text
MRI coupled with magnetic resonance cholangiopancreatography (MRCP) is now frequently used for diagnosis and can show masses in the pancreas as well as provide more optimal visualization of ductal changes consistent with chronic pancreatitis.
Generated Simplification
An MRI, which is a type of scan that uses magnets to take detailed pictures of the inside of your body, is often combined with another special scan called MRCP. The MRCP focuses on the pancreas and nearby ducts (small tubes that carry fluids). Together, these scans can help doctors find growths (masses) in the pancreas and see changes in the ducts that might be linked to a condition called chronic pancreatitis. Chronic pancreatitis is a long-term inflammation of the pancreas.
Reference Simplification
MRCP shows the bile and pancreatic ducts more clearly than does CT.

Example 148 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 148
Source Text
During childhood, she suffered from partially collapsed lungs twice, had pneumonia four to five times a year, as well as a ruptured appendix and a tonsillar cyst.
Generated Simplification
When she was a child, she had several serious health problems: - Her lungs partially collapsed two times. This means part of her lungs stopped working properly for a while. - She had pneumonia four to five times each year. Pneumonia is a lung infection that can make it hard to breathe and cause fever and coughing. - Her appendix burst (this is called a ruptured appendix), which can cause severe belly pain and needs emergency treatment. - She also had a cyst on her tonsils. A cyst is a small, fluid-filled lump that can sometimes cause discomfort or other problems. These are all significant health issues that may have affected her overall health as a child.
Reference Simplification
She had a collapsed lung twice, had pneumonia four or five times each year and had problems with her appendix and tonsils.

Complicated UTIs are more difficult to treat and usually requires more aggressive evaluation, treatment and follow-up.

Some urinary tract infections (UTIs) are harder to treat. These are called complicated UTIs. - Treating a complicated UTI often takes more time and stronger treatments. - Your doctor may need to do extra tests to understand the problem better. - After treatment, you might need follow-up care to make sure the infection is completely gone. If you think you have a UTI, talk to your doctor. They can decide the best way to help you.

A complicated UTI is more difficult to treat and usually needs more aggressive evaluation, treatment, and follow-up.

Source Text
Myoclonus can vary in amplitude, frequency, and distribution.
Generated Simplification
Myoclonus means sudden, quick muscle jerks that you can’t control. These jerks can: - Be small or large (this is called amplitude). - Happen often or only once in a while (this is called frequency). - Affect one part of your body or several areas (this is called distribution). If you notice muscle jerks, talk to your doctor. They can help figure out what’s causing them and what to do next.
Reference Simplification
Myoclonus can be mild or severe. Muscles may jerk quickly or slowly, rhythmically or not. Myoclonus may occur once in a while or frequently.

Example 162 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 162
Source Text
Nonconvulsive status epilepticus is being increasingly recognized as a cause of altered mental status in ICU patients.
Generated Simplification
Nonconvulsive status epilepticus (NCSE) is a condition that can affect the brain. It happens when someone has ongoing seizure activity, but without the shaking or jerking movements that people usually associate with seizures. Doctors are finding that NCSE is more common than they once thought, especially in patients in the intensive care unit (ICU). It can cause changes in how a person thinks or behaves, which is called "altered mental status." If you or a loved one is in the ICU and showing unusual confusion, drowsiness, or other changes in awareness, doctors may check for NCSE as a possible cause.
Reference Simplification
People in ICUs may have seizures that do not cause convulsions (called nonconvulsive seizures). These seizures can cause delirium, but the seizures may not be recognized because they do not cause convulsions or other typical symptoms of seizures. If the seizures are not recognized, they may not be treated appropriately and promptly.
BERTScore
Precision
0.086
Recall
0.618
F1
0.34

Gout can present in a number of ways, although the most usual is a recurrent attack of acute inflammatory arthritis ( a red, tender, hot, swollen joint ).
Generated Simplification
Gout can show up in different ways, but the most common sign is a sudden flare-up of joint pain and swelling. This is called acute inflammatory arthritis. During a gout attack, the affected joint may: - Look red - Feel tender or painful to touch - Feel hot - Become swollen If you notice these symptoms, it’s a good idea to talk to your doctor. They can help figure out if it’s gout and discuss treatment options with you.
Reference Simplification
Gout can present in a number of ways, although the most common is a recurrent attack of intense inflammatory arthritis ( a red, tender, hot, swollen joint ).

Example 165 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 165
Source Text
Familial Alzheimer 's disease and familial Parkinson 's disease are two accelerated aging diseases associated with aged individuals; they affect only one tissue and can be classified as unimodal progeroid syndromes.
Generated Simplification
Some families may have a higher chance of developing certain conditions like Alzheimer’s disease or Parkinson’s disease. These are diseases that usually happen as people get older. - **Familial Alzheimer’s disease** and **familial Parkinson’s disease** are types of these conditions that can run in families. - These diseases mainly affect one part of the body. For example, they mostly impact the brain. - Because they are linked to aging and happen faster than usual, they are sometimes called "accelerated aging diseases." If you have questions about your family history or risks for these conditions, it’s a good idea to talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
Familial Alzheimer 's disease and familial Parkinson 's disease, an accelerated aging disease associated with aged individuals, affects only one tissue: it can be classified as an unimodal progeroid syndrome.

Example 167 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 167
Source Text
Olfactory seizures, manifested as an aura of foul smells (rotten eggs, burnt meat), indicate temporal lobe involvement and suggest HSV encephalitis.
Generated Simplification
Sometimes, seizures can cause unusual smells that aren't really there. These smells are often described as bad, like rotten eggs or burnt meat. This type of seizure is called an "olfactory seizure." Olfactory seizures can be a sign that a part of the brain called the temporal lobe is affected. In some cases, they may suggest a condition called HSV encephalitis, which is a serious brain infection caused by the herpes simplex virus. If you or someone you know experiences these symptoms, it’s important to talk to a doctor right away. Early treatment can make a big difference.
Reference Simplification
People also have seizures, sometimes accompanied by strange smells (such as rotten eggs), vivid flashbacks, or sudden, intense emotions.

Source Text
Endoscopy means looking inside and typically refers to looking inside the body for medical reasons using an endoscope, an instrument used to examine the interior of a hollow organ or cavity of the body.

An endoscopy is a medical procedure that lets doctors look inside your body. They use a special tool called an endoscope, which is a thin, flexible tube with a light and camera on the end. This tool helps them see the inside of certain parts of your body, like your stomach or other hollow areas, to check for any problems.

Pain is usually felt in the area between the anus and the genitals (called the perineum). However, some people may feel the pain in other places, such as: - The tip of the penis - The lower back - The testicles If you are experiencing this kind of pain, it’s important to talk to a healthcare provider. They can help figure out the cause and suggest the right treatment.

Example 171 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 171
Source Text
Pain is usually felt over the inflamed site, but referred pain is possible.
Generated Simplification
Pain is usually felt in the area where there is inflammation, which means the spot is swollen or irritated. However, sometimes the pain can be felt in a different part of the body. This is called "referred pain." For example, if there is a problem with your liver, you might feel pain in your right shoulder instead of just near the liver.
Reference Simplification
The pain is usually felt in the chest wall right over the site of the inflammation or infection that caused the effusion.

Example 172 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 172
Source Text
Pyelonephritis is treated more aggressively than a simple bladder infection using either a longer course of oral antibiotics or intravenous antibiotics.
Generated Simplification
A kidney infection, also called pyelonephritis, is treated more seriously than a regular bladder infection. - For a kidney infection, you may need to take antibiotics for a longer time. Antibiotics are medicines that fight infections caused by bacteria. - In some cases, the antibiotics might need to be given through a vein (this is called intravenous or IV antibiotics). This is usually done in a hospital. Your doctor will decide the best treatment for you based on your condition.
Reference Simplification
Pyelonephritis ( kidney infection ) is treated more aggressively than a simple bladder infection using either a longer course of oral antibiotics or intravenous antibiotics.

The outcome is frequently coma, with over 90 % of patients with severe DAI never regaining consciousness.

Severe DAI (diffuse axonal injury) is a type of serious brain injury. In most cases, people with severe DAI fall into a coma, which means they are unconscious and cannot wake up or respond to their surroundings. Unfortunately, more than 90% of people with this condition do not wake up from the coma.

Example 174 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 174
Source Text
Patients are taller than average for age and family; arm span exceeds height. Arachnodactyly (disproportionately long, thin digits) is noticeable, often by the thumb sign (the distal phalanx of the thumb protrudes beyond the edge of the clenched fist).
Generated Simplification
Some patients may grow taller than what is typical for their age or family. Their arm span (the distance from fingertip to fingertip when arms are stretched out) may also be longer than their height. They might have very long, thin fingers, a condition called arachnodactyly. One way to notice this is through the "thumb sign." This happens when the tip of the thumb sticks out past the edge of a closed fist.
Reference Simplification
People with Marfan syndrome are taller than expected for their age and family. Their arm span (the distance between fingertips when the arms are outstretched) is greater than their height. Their fingers are long and thin.

Example 175 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 175
Source Text
Patients may present with systemic symptoms such as fever (usually low - grade), fatigue, malaise, unexplained weight loss, and sweats. Some patients are initially diagnosed as having fever of unknown origin.
Generated Simplification
You might experience symptoms that affect your whole body, such as: - A mild fever (your body feels warmer than usual). - Feeling very tired or worn out. - A general feeling of being unwell. - Losing weight without trying. - Sweating more than usual, especially at night. Sometimes, doctors may not know the exact cause of these symptoms right away. In these cases, they might call it a "fever of unknown origin," which means they need to do more tests to figure out what’s causing it. If you notice these symptoms, it’s important to talk to your doctor so they can help find the cause and decide on the best treatment.
Reference Simplification
People may have fever and feel tired and generally unwell. They may lose weight unintentionally and sweat more than usual.

Example 176 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 176
Source Text
Patients suspected of having stomach cancer should have endoscopy with multiple biopsies and brush cytology. Occasionally, a biopsy limited to the mucosa misses tumor tissue in the submucosa.
Generated Simplification
If your doctor thinks you might have stomach cancer, they will usually recommend a test called an endoscopy. During this test, a thin, flexible tube with a camera is passed down your throat to look at your stomach. Here’s what happens during the test: - The doctor will take small samples of tissue from your stomach lining. This is called a biopsy. - They may also use a small brush to collect cells for testing. Sometimes, cancer can be deeper in the stomach wall, below the surface layer (called the submucosa). In these cases, the biopsy might not catch it. If your doctor has concerns, they may suggest other tests or follow-up procedures. Talk to your doctor if you have questions about the test or your results.
Reference Simplification
Endoscopy (an examination in which a flexible tube is used to visualize the inside of the digestive tract) is the best diagnostic procedure. It allows a doctor to view the stomach directly, to check for Helicobacter pylori, and to remove tissue samples for examination under a microscope (biopsy).
BERTScore
Precision
0.004
Recall
0.314

0.156

Example 177 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 177
Source Text
Rates of asymptomatic bacteria in the urine increase with age from two to seven percent in women of child bearing age to as high as 50 % in elderly women in care homes.
Generated Simplification
As women get older, it becomes more common to have bacteria in their urine without any symptoms. - For women who can have children, this happens in about 2 to 7 out of every 100 women. - For older women living in care homes, it can happen in up to 50 out of every 100 women. This means that as women age, the chance of having bacteria in the urine without feeling sick increases.
Reference Simplification
Rates of asymptomatic bacteria in the urine increase with age from 2 % to 7 % in women of childbearing age to as high as 50 % in elderly women in care homes.

Example 179 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 179
Source Text
Seven days of the oral fluoroquinolone ciprofloxacin is typically used in areas where the resistance rate is less than 10 %.
Generated Simplification
Doctors often prescribe a medicine called ciprofloxacin, which is taken by mouth, for 7 days to treat certain infections. This is usually done in places where less than 10% of the bacteria causing the infection are resistant to the medicine. If you have questions about whether this treatment is right for you, talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
Seven days of the oral fluoroquinolone ciprofloxacin is typically used in geographic areas where the resistance rate is less than 10 %.

Example 180 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 180
Source Text
The study also indicated that increased ejaculation through masturbation rather than intercourse would be more helpful as intercourse is associated with diseases ( STDs ) that may increase the risk of cancer instead.
Generated Simplification
Research suggests that ejaculating more often may help reduce the risk of certain health problems. Masturbation may be a safer way to do this compared to sexual intercourse. This is because sexual intercourse can sometimes lead to sexually transmitted diseases (STDs), which might increase the risk of cancer. If you have questions about this or your health, it’s a good idea to talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
The study also indicated that this would be more helpful than ejaculation through sexual intercourse because intercourse can transmit diseases that may increase the risk of cancer instead.

Example 181 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 181
Source Text
Waist circumference and BP
Generated Simplification
**Waist Size and Blood Pressure** Your waist size and blood pressure are connected to your health. Here’s what you need to know: - **Waist Circumference**: This means the measurement around your waist. A larger waist size can be a sign of extra fat around your belly. This type of fat can increase your risk of health problems like heart disease and diabetes. - **Blood Pressure (BP)**: This is the force of your blood pushing against the walls of your blood vessels. If your blood pressure is too high, it can strain your heart and lead to serious problems like heart attacks or strokes. - **The Connection**: If you have a larger waist size, it may raise your blood pressure. This happens because extra belly fat can affect how your body handles blood flow and hormones. **What You Can Do**: - Talk to your doctor about your waist size and blood pressure. They can help you understand your risks and what steps to take. - Eating healthy, staying active, and maintaining a healthy weight can help lower your blood pressure and reduce belly fat. If you’re unsure about your waist size or blood pressure, ask your healthcare provider to check them for you.
Reference Simplification
Waist circumference Blood pressure measurement

-0.312
Recall
0.184
F1
-0.075

Example 184 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 184
Source Text
A palpable bladder may be present with outlet obstruction. The costovertebral angle may be tender if the kidney is acutely enlarged.
Generated Simplification
You might feel your bladder (the organ that holds urine) if there is a blockage that makes it hard for urine to flow out. If your kidney is suddenly swollen or enlarged, you might feel pain or tenderness in the area where your lower back meets your side. This spot is called the costovertebral angle. If you notice these symptoms, it’s important to talk to a healthcare provider. They can help figure out what’s causing the problem and recommend the right treatment.
Reference Simplification
If acute kidney injury is caused by a blockage (an obstruction), the backup of urine within the kidneys causes the drainage system to stretch (a condition called hydronephrosis â see Figure: Hydronephrosis: A Distended Kidney).

Example 185 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 185
Source Text
A peripheral ring scotoma (detectable by visual field testing) widens gradually, and central vision may also be affected in advanced cases.
Generated Simplification
A peripheral ring scotoma is a type of vision problem. It means there is a ring-shaped area around your central vision where you cannot see clearly. This can be found during a special eye test called a visual field test. Over time, this problem can get worse, and the blurry or missing area may grow larger. In more serious cases, it can also affect your central vision, which is the part of your sight you use to see things directly in front of you. If you notice changes in your vision, it’s important to talk to your eye doctor. They can check your eyes and discuss what steps to take next.
Reference Simplification
In the late stages of the disease, the person typically has only a small area of central vision and possibly no peripheral vision remaining (tunnel vision). Sometimes in late stages the macula, the area of the retina responsible for central vision, swells. This swelling is called macular edema and affects central vision.

Example 186 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 186
Source Text
for kids Bruises in sports
Generated Simplification
When kids play sports, it’s common for them to get bruises. A bruise happens when small blood vessels under the skin break after a bump or hit. This causes the skin to look purple, blue, or black. Over time, the bruise will change colors, like green or yellow, as it heals. ### What to Do for a Bruise: - **Rest the area:** If the bruise is on a leg or arm, try to avoid using it too much. - **Ice the bruise:** Put a cold pack or a bag of ice wrapped in a cloth on the bruise for 15–20 minutes. This helps reduce swelling and pain. - **Elevate the area:** If possible, raise the bruised area above the level of the heart. For example, if it’s on the leg, have your child lie down and prop their leg up on a pillow. ### When to See a Doctor: Most bruises are not serious and will heal on their own. However, you should contact a doctor if: - The bruise is very large or painful. - The bruise doesn’t improve after a couple of weeks. - Your child gets bruises often without a clear reason. - The bruise is near the eye and causes vision problems. - There is swelling or trouble moving the area around the bruise. ### Preventing Bruises in Sports: - Make sure your child wears the right protective gear, like pads or helmets, for their sport. - Teach your child to play safely and follow the rules of the game. If you’re unsure about a bruise or how to treat it, it’s always a good idea to talk to your child’s doctor.
Reference Simplification
for kids Bruises in sports Fake bruises as fashion

-0.433
Recall
0.158
F1
-0.153

Example 187 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 187
Source Text
Monozygotic twins have a concordance of about 45 %.
Generated Simplification
Identical twins (also called monozygotic twins) share the same genetic makeup because they come from the same fertilized egg. This means they are often very similar in many ways. When doctors talk about "concordance," they mean how often both twins have the same condition or trait. For identical twins, this happens about 45% of the time. In other words, if one twin has a certain condition, there is a 45% chance the other twin will have it too. If you have questions about what this means for you or your family, it’s a good idea to talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
An identical twin whose co-twin has schizophrenia has about a 50 % risk of developing schizophrenia.

The findings usually include: - Swelling of the thin, clear tissue that covers the white part of your eye (this is called conjunctival edema). - Redness in the eye (this is called hyperemia). - A discharge, which may look like mucus or pus, coming from the eye. If you notice these symptoms, it’s important to talk to a healthcare provider. They can help figure out what’s causing the problem and recommend the right treatment.

Example 189 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 189
Source Text
Advanced disease can lead to destruction of supporting cartilage, causing floppy ears, saddle nose, pectus excavatum, and visual, auditory, and vestibular abnormalities.
Generated Simplification
When a disease becomes severe, it can damage the cartilage in the body. Cartilage is the flexible tissue that helps support and shape parts of your body, like your ears and nose. If this tissue breaks down, it can cause: - **Floppy ears**: The ears may lose their firmness and droop. - **Saddle nose**: The bridge of the nose may collapse, making it look flat or sunken. - **Pectus excavatum**: This is when the chest appears sunken or caved in. - **Problems with vision, hearing, and balance**: These issues can happen if the disease affects areas that control sight, sound, or balance. If you notice any of these changes, it’s important to talk to your doctor. They can help figure out what’s causing the problem and discuss treatment options.
Reference Simplification
Eventually, the supporting cartilage can be damaged, resulting in floppy ears, a sloping saddle nose, and a hollow at the lower part of the chest (pectus excavatum). The nerve in the inner ear can be affected, causing eventual problems with balance and hearing, and eventually vision problems can occur.

Example 191 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 191
Source Text
Nephritis is inflammation of the kidneys and may involve the glomeruli, tubules, or interstitial tissue surrounding the glomeruli and tubules.
Generated Simplification
Nephritis means swelling or inflammation in the kidneys. This can affect different parts of the kidneys, such as: - The glomeruli, which are tiny filters that clean your blood. - The tubules, which help remove waste and extra fluid. - The tissue around these areas. If you think you might have a kidney problem, talk to your doctor. They can help figure out what’s going on and what treatment you might need.
Reference Simplification
It may involve the glomeruli, tubules, or interstitial tissue surrounding the glomeruli and tubules. Nephritis is often caused by infections, and toxins, but is most commonly caused by autoimmune disorders that affect the major organs like kidneys. Nephritis can produce glomerular injury, by disturbing the glomerular structure with inflammatory cell proliferation.

Example 192 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 192
Source Text
Strabismus may be infantile or acquired.
Generated Simplification
Strabismus is a condition where the eyes do not line up properly. This means that one eye may look straight ahead while the other eye turns in a different direction, such as inward, outward, up, or down. - Some people are born with strabismus. This is called infantile strabismus. - Others develop it later in life. This is called acquired strabismus. If you notice that your child or you have this issue, it’s important to talk to a doctor. They can help figure out the cause and discuss treatment options.
Reference Simplification
Strabismus may appear in the first few months of life or later in childhood, depending on the cause.

Example 193 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 193
Source Text
Nasogastric suction
Generated Simplification
A nasogastric suction is a medical procedure where a thin, flexible tube is inserted through your nose and down into your stomach. This tube is used to remove fluids, air, or other substances from your stomach. Here’s how it works and why it might be needed: - **How it works**: The tube is gently passed through your nose, down your throat, and into your stomach. It is connected to a machine that creates suction to pull out unwanted contents from your stomach. - **Why it’s done**: This procedure is often used if you have a blockage in your stomach or intestines, if your stomach is not emptying properly, or if you have swallowed something harmful. It can also help reduce nausea, vomiting, or bloating caused by too much fluid or gas in your stomach. - **What to expect**: You may feel some discomfort when the tube is being placed, but the process is usually quick. Your healthcare provider will guide you through it and make sure you’re as comfortable as possible. If you have questions or concerns about this procedure, talk to your doctor. They can explain why it’s needed in your case and what to expect during and after the procedure.
Reference Simplification
Suction via nasogastric tube.

Recall
0.378

Example 194 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 194
Source Text
Symptoms and signs of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis typically develop over 6 mo to several years and include dyspnea on exertion and nonproductive cough.
Generated Simplification
Symptoms of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) usually appear slowly over time, often taking 6 months to several years to develop. Common symptoms include: - Feeling short of breath, especially when you are active or moving around. - A dry cough that doesn’t bring up mucus. If you notice these symptoms, it’s important to talk to a doctor.
Reference Simplification
Symptoms depend on the extent of the lung damage, the rate at which the disease progresses, and whether complications, such as lung infections and right-sided heart failure (cor pulmonale) develop. The main symptoms start insidiously as shortness of breath during exertion, cough, and diminished stamina. In most people, symptoms worsen over a period ranging from about 6 months to several years.

Example 197 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 197
Source Text
Symptoms include rectal itching, a cloudy rectal discharge, bleeding, and constipation âall of varying severity. Examination with a proctoscope may detect erythema or mucopurulent exudate on the rectal wall.
Generated Simplification
Symptoms you might notice include: - Itching around the rectum (the opening where stool leaves the body). - A cloudy fluid coming from the rectum. - Bleeding from the rectum. - Trouble passing stool or feeling constipated. These symptoms can range from mild to more severe. To check for the cause, a doctor might use a tool called a proctoscope. This is a small device that helps the doctor look inside the rectum. During the exam, the doctor might see: - Redness or irritation inside the rectum. - A thick or cloudy substance on the rectal wall. If you have these symptoms, it’s important to talk to a healthcare provider. They can help find out what’s causing the problem and recommend treatment.
Reference Simplification
This infection usually causes no symptoms, but it may make bowel movements painful. Other symptoms include constipation, itching, bleeding, and a discharge from the rectum. The area around the anus may become red and raw, and stool may be coated with mucus and pus. When a doctor examines the rectum with a viewing tube (anoscope), mucus and pus may be visible on the wall of the rectum.

Example 200 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 200
Source Text
Obstetric ultrasonography should be done to rule out hydatidiform mole and multifetal pregnancy.
Generated Simplification
An ultrasound during pregnancy is important to check for certain conditions. - One condition is a hydatidiform mole, which happens when a pregnancy develops abnormally and does not result in a baby. - Another condition is a multifetal pregnancy, which means being pregnant with more than one baby, like twins or triplets. Your doctor may recommend an ultrasound to look for these issues and make sure everything is progressing as it should.
Reference Simplification
Ultrasonography is usually done to determine whether women have a hydatidiform mole (a disorder that initially resembles pregnancy) or more than one fetus (multiple births).

Example 201 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 201
Source Text
IV fluids.
Generated Simplification
IV fluids are liquids that are given to you through a small tube placed in a vein, usually in your arm. "IV" stands for "intravenous," which means "into the vein." These fluids are used to help your body when you need extra hydration, nutrients, or medicine. For example, they might be given if you are dehydrated, have lost a lot of fluids, or need medicine that works best when delivered directly into your bloodstream. If you have questions about IV fluids or why they are being recommended for you, talk to your doctor or nurse. They can explain how they will help and what to expect.
Reference Simplification
Fluids given by vein

Example 203 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 203
Source Text
Bronchiectasis is best considered the common end-point of various disorders that cause chronic airway inflammation.
Generated Simplification
Bronchiectasis happens when the airways in your lungs become damaged after long-term swelling and irritation. This damage can be caused by different health problems that lead to ongoing inflammation in the airways.
Reference Simplification
The most common cause is severe or repeated respiratory infections, which are particularly likely in people who have one or more of the following: Cystic fibrosis; Other hereditary disorders, such as primary ciliary dyskinesia, in which the ability to clear the airways is impaired; Immunodeficiency disorders; Certain autoimmune disorders, such as rheumatoid arthritis, ulcerative colitis, or Sjogren syndrome; Mechanical factors, such as airway blockage caused by an inhaled object, chronically enlarged lymph nodes, changes after lung surgery, or a lung tumor; Inhaling toxic substances that injure the airways, such as noxious fumes, gases, smoke (including tobacco smoke), and injurious dust (for example, silica and coal dust).

A water crisis is a situation where the available supply of potable, clean water within an area is less than the demand for it. The Food and Agriculture Organization ( FAO ) estimates that by 2025, 1.9 billion people will be living in countries or regions with total water scarcity, and two-thirds of the world population could be under stress conditions.

The most common causes overall are Corneal abrasion; Foreign bodies.

The most common reasons for this problem are: - **Corneal abrasion**: This means a scratch or injury on the clear, front part of your eye (the cornea). - **Foreign bodies**: This happens when something like dust, dirt, or a small object gets into your eye. If you think you have either of these issues, it’s important to see a doctor. They can check your eye and recommend the right treatment.

Corneal disorders are the most common causes of eye pain overall, particularly Corneal scratches (corneal abrasions); Foreign objects.

Example 210 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 210
Source Text
A corneal ulcer begins as a corneal epithelial defect that stains with fluorescein and an underlying dull, grayish, circumscribed superficial opacity (representing an infiltrate).
Generated Simplification
A corneal ulcer is a sore or open wound on the clear, front part of your eye, called the cornea. It starts when the outer layer of the cornea, known as the epithelium, gets damaged. This damage can be seen using a special dye called fluorescein, which highlights the affected area. Underneath the damaged spot, there may be a dull, grayish patch that looks cloudy. This cloudy area is caused by cells from your immune system gathering there to fight off infection or irritation.
Reference Simplification
The ulcer often appears as a white or dull and grayish spot on the cornea.

Example 212 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 212
Source Text
From May to October 2000, an outbreak of tularemia in Martha 's Vineyard resulted in one fatality, and brought the interest of the CDC as a potential investigative ground for aerosolized Francisella tularensis.

Between May and October 2000, there was an outbreak of a disease called tularemia on Martha's Vineyard. Tularemia is a rare infection caused by bacteria called *Francisella tularensis*. During this outbreak, one person died. The situation caught the attention of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), which looked into whether the bacteria might have spread through the air (aerosolized).
Reference Simplification
It brought the interest of the CDC as a potential investigative ground for aerosolized Francisella tularensis.

Example 215 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 215
Source Text
90.9 % of these were classified as having been done by `` curettage '' (suction-aspiration, dilation and curettage, dilation and evacuation), 7.7 % by `` medical '' means (mifepristone), 0.4 % by `` intrauterine instillation '' (saline or prostaglandin), and 1.0 % by `` other '' (including hysterotomy and hysterectomy).
Generated Simplification
Most of these procedures (90.9%) were done using a method called "curettage." This includes techniques like suction-aspiration, dilation and curettage (D&C), or dilation and evacuation (D&E). A smaller number (7.7%) were done using medicines, such as mifepristone, which is a pill that helps end a pregnancy. Only 0.4% were done by placing a liquid, like saline (saltwater) or prostaglandin (a hormone-like substance), into the uterus. The remaining 1% were done using other methods, such as surgery to remove the pregnancy through procedures like hysterotomy or hysterectomy. If you have questions about these methods, talk to your doctor for more information.
Reference Simplification
90.9 % were done by curettage, 7.7 % were medical abortions (using drugs, mifepristone in most cases), 0.4 % by `` intrauterine instillation '' (saline or prostaglandin), and 1.0 % by `` other '' (including hysterotomy and hysterectomy).

Example 216 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 216
Source Text
About 10 % of patients have dermatitis herpetiformis, an intensely pruritic papulovesicular rash that is symmetrically distributed over the extensor areas of the elbows, knees, buttocks, shoulders, and scalp.
Generated Simplification
Around 10% of people with this condition develop dermatitis herpetiformis. This is a very itchy skin rash that looks like small bumps or blisters. The rash usually appears on both sides of the body in the same places. Common areas include: - The backs of the elbows - The knees - The buttocks - The shoulders - The scalp If you think you might have this rash, talk to your doctor. They can help figure out what’s causing it and suggest the right treatment.
Reference Simplification
About 10 % of people with celiac disease develop a painful, itchy rash with small blistersâa disease called dermatitis herpetiformis.

Example 217 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 217
Source Text
Anteroposterior, lateral, and usually oblique x-rays are taken. A fracture, if present, is usually visible on the lateral view. X-rays may be normal if only the tendon is torn.
Generated Simplification
Doctors usually take several types of X-ray images to check for injuries. These include: - A front-to-back view (anteroposterior). - A side view (lateral). - Often, an angled view (oblique). If a bone is broken (fractured), it is usually easiest to see on the side view. However, if only a tendon—a strong band of tissue that connects muscle to bone—is torn, the X-rays might look normal.
Reference Simplification
X-rays are usually taken at several different angles to check for a fracture.

Because the heterozygote survives , so does the HgbS allele survive at a rate much higher than the mutation rate ( see and refs in Sickle-cell disease ) .
Generated Simplification
The HgbS allele, which is linked to sickle cell disease, continues to exist in the population because people who inherit just one copy of this gene (called "heterozygotes") are more likely to survive. This survival advantage helps the gene stay more common than it would if it only appeared due to new mutations.

Example 222 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 222
Source Text
Crohn disease should be suspected in a patient with inflammatory or obstructive symptoms or in a patient without prominent GI symptoms but with perianal fistulas or abscesses or with otherwise unexplained arthritis, erythema nodosum, fever, anemia, or (in a child) stunted growth. A family history of Crohn disease also increases the index of suspicion.
Generated Simplification
Crohn's disease is a condition that can affect the digestive system. It might be considered if someone has certain symptoms or health issues. Here are some signs that could suggest Crohn's disease: - Ongoing problems with inflammation or blockages in the digestive system. - Painful openings or infections around the anus (called perianal fistulas or abscesses). - Unexplained joint pain or swelling (arthritis). - Red, tender bumps on the skin (erythema nodosum). - Fever without a clear cause. - Low levels of red blood cells (anemia), which can make you feel tired or weak. - In children, slower growth than expected. If someone in your family has Crohn's disease, it may increase the chance of having it too. If you have any of these symptoms or concerns, it’s important to talk to a doctor. They can help figure out if Crohn's disease or something else might be causing the problem.
Reference Simplification
A doctor may suspect Crohn disease in a person with recurring crampy abdominal pain and diarrhea, particularly if the person has a family history of Crohn disease or a history of problems around the anus.
BERTScore
Precision
0.049
Recall
0.289
F1
0.167

Some people with Tourette's syndrome might not notice the feeling that comes before a tic. This feeling is called a "premonitory urge." It’s like a warning signal your body gives you before the tic happens, such as a sense of tension or discomfort. Not everyone with Tourette's experiences this, and that’s okay. If you have questions about this, talk to your doctor.

However, many also go on to form abscesses, which require surgical drainage; an extensive procedure is done with accompanying IV antibiotics.
Generated Simplification
Sometimes, these infections can turn into abscesses. An abscess is a swollen area filled with pus caused by an infection. To treat an abscess, a doctor may need to perform a procedure to drain the pus. This is a more involved treatment and is often done along with giving antibiotics through an IV (a tube that delivers medicine directly into your vein).

Abscesses must be drained surgically.

Example 225 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 225
Source Text
In mammals, it consists of the bony labyrinth, a hollow cavity in the temporal bone of the skull with a system of passages comprising two main functional parts: The inner ear is found in all vertebrates, with substantial variations in form and function.
Generated Simplification
The inner ear is a part of the body found in all animals with backbones, including humans. It is located inside the skull, within a hard bone called the temporal bone. The inner ear has two main jobs: - It helps with hearing. - It helps with balance, which is how your body stays steady and upright. The shape and function of the inner ear can be different in different animals, but it always plays an important role in these two areas.
Reference Simplification
In mammals, it consists of two main working parts inside a bony labyrinth: The inner ear is found in all vertebrates, with many variations in form and function.

The recommended treatment is a medicine called itraconazole. You take it by mouth as a 200 mg dose once a day. Example 235 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 235
Source Text
Among spinal subdural abscesses, methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus is the most common organism involved. Rarely parasites can cause abscesses and this is more common in the developing world.
Generated Simplification
An infection called a spinal subdural abscess happens when pus collects in the space around the spinal cord. This can be caused by germs, such as bacteria or, in rare cases, parasites. - The most common germ that causes this type of infection is a bacteria called *Staphylococcus aureus*. When this bacteria can be treated with a common antibiotic called methicillin, it is referred to as "methicillin-sensitive." - Parasites, which are tiny organisms that live in or on other living things, can also cause these abscesses, but this is very rare. It happens more often in parts of the world where healthcare resources may be limited. If you have concerns about infections or symptoms related to your spine, it’s important to talk to a healthcare professional.
Reference Simplification
Among spinal subdural abscesses, methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus is the most common organism involved.
BERTScore
Precision
0.134
Recall
0.537
F1
0.329

Example 236 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 236
Source Text
Periungual warts are more common among patients who bite their nails or who have occupations where their hands are chronically wet such as dishwashers and bartenders. Genital warts manifest as discrete flat to broad-based smooth to velvety papules to rough and pedunculated excrescences on the perineal, perirectal, labial, and penile areas. Perirectal warts often itch.
Generated Simplification
Warts can appear in different areas of the body and may look different depending on where they are. ### Warts Around the Fingernails (Periungual Warts) - These warts are more common in people who bite their nails. - They are also more likely to happen if you have a job where your hands are often wet, like washing dishes or working as a bartender. ### Genital Warts - These warts can show up in areas like the genitals, around the anus, or nearby skin. - They can look different: - Some are flat and smooth. - Others are raised, rough, or have a velvety texture. - Some may even have a stalk-like shape. - Warts around the anus (perirectal warts) can often cause itching. If you notice any of these types of warts, it’s a good idea to talk to a healthcare provider. They can help with diagnosis and treatment options.
Reference Simplification
Also called venereal warts or condylomata acuminata, genital warts occur on the penis, anus, vulva, vagina, and cervix. They can be flat, smooth, and velvety or irregular, bumpy growths often with the texture of a small cauliflower. Warts around the anus often itch.
BERTScore
Precision
0.072
Recall
0.232
F1
0.152

S2 2 Active voice is used throughout the simplification, making it clearer.

Example 237 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 237
Source Text
The barking cough and stridor may no longer be prominent at this stage. 85 % of children presenting to the emergency department have mild disease; severe croup is rare ( < 1 % ).
Generated Simplification
At this point, the barking cough and noisy breathing (called stridor) may not be as noticeable. Most children—about 85%—who come to the emergency room with croup have a mild form of the illness. Severe croup is very rare and happens in less than 1% of cases.
Reference Simplification
The barking cough and wheezing does not always happen at this stage. 85 % of children going to the emergency department have mild disease.

Example 238 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 238
Source Text
The risk of overdose is increased if flunitrazepam is taken in combination with CNS depressants such as alcohol and opiates.
Generated Simplification
Taking flunitrazepam with other substances that slow down the brain and body can increase the risk of an overdose. These substances include: - Alcohol - Opiates (strong pain medicines like morphine or heroin) An overdose can be very dangerous. If you are taking flunitrazepam, avoid using these substances together. Talk to your doctor if you have any questions or concerns.
Reference Simplification
The overdose will be worse if flunitrazepam is taken with depressants, like alcohol or opiates.

Example 239 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 239
Source Text
The Intersex Society of North America stated that `` For decades, doctors have thought it necessary to treat intersex with a concealment-centered approach, one that features downplaying intersex as much as possible, even to the point of lying to patients about their conditions.
Generated Simplification
For many years, doctors often used a "concealment-centered approach" to treat intersex people. This means they tried to hide or minimize the fact that someone was intersex. In some cases, this even included not fully telling patients the truth about their condition.
Reference Simplification
About 1 in 100 babies are born with an intersex condition, and 1 in 1000 babies have an intersex condition that the medical community declares needs surgery, in order adhere to binary sex/gender ideals.

Example 240 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 240
Source Text
Biopsy of the lung is usually not indicated unless the pulmonary hypertension is thought to be due to an underlying interstitial lung disease; further, lung biopsies are fraught with risks of bleeding due to the high intrapulmonary blood pressure.
Generated Simplification
A lung biopsy is not usually needed unless doctors think the pulmonary hypertension (high blood pressure in the lungs) is caused by another lung condition called interstitial lung disease. However, lung biopsies can be risky because they may cause bleeding. This is due to the high blood pressure in the lungs. If a biopsy is being considered, your doctor will carefully weigh the risks and benefits and discuss them with you.
Reference Simplification
Biopsy of the lung is usually not indicated unless the pulmonary hypertension is thought to be due to an underlying interstitial lung disease.

Example 241 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 241
Source Text
Epidemiological studies can only go to prove that an agent could have caused, but not that it did cause, an effect in any particular case: In United States law, epidemiology alone can not prove that a causal association does not exist in general.
Generated Simplification
Studies that look at how diseases spread and what might cause them (called epidemiological studies) can show that something *might* cause a certain effect. However, these studies cannot prove that it *did* cause the effect in a specific person. In the United States, these studies alone cannot prove that there is no connection between a possible cause and an effect in general.
Reference Simplification
Epidemiological studies can only go to prove that an agent could have caused disease in population, but not that it did cause, an effect in any particular case.

Example 242 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 242
Source Text
Signs occur along a continuum: no changes to varicose veins (rare) to edema to stasis dermatitis on the lower legs and at the ankles, with or without ulceration (see Table: Clinical Classification of Chronic Venous Insufficiency). Venous stasis dermatitis consists of reddish brown hyperpigmentation, induration, venous ectasia, lipodermatosclerosis (fibrosing subcutaneous panniculitis), and venous stasis ulcers.
Generated Simplification
Signs of this condition can range from no visible changes in the veins (this is rare) to more noticeable problems, such as: - Swelling in the legs (called edema). - Skin changes on the lower legs and ankles, known as stasis dermatitis. This can happen with or without open sores (ulcers). Stasis dermatitis is a skin condition caused by poor blood flow in the veins. It can include: - Reddish-brown discoloration of the skin. - Thickened or hardened skin. - Enlarged veins that are visible under the skin. - A condition called lipodermatosclerosis, where the fat under the skin becomes inflamed and scarred. - Open sores on the skin, called venous stasis ulcers. If you notice any of these changes, it’s important to talk to your doctor. They can help diagnose the problem and recommend treatment.
Reference Simplification
The discoloration is caused by red blood cells that escape from swollen (distended) veins into the skin. The discolored skin is vulnerable, and even a minor injury, such as that from scratching or a bump, can break it open, resulting in an ulcer.

Precision
0.128
Recall
0.218
F1
0.174

Source Text
Most commonly found as small circular, double-stranded DNA molecules in bacteria, plasmids are sometimes present in archaea and eukaryotic organisms.

Plasmids are small, circular pieces of DNA. They are most often found in bacteria. Sometimes, they can also be found in other organisms, like certain types of single-celled organisms (archaea) and more complex organisms (eukaryotes).

Example 245 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 245
Source Text
A common finding during physical examination is a symmetry or a dominant mass âa mass distinctly different from the surrounding breast tissue. Diffuse fibrotic changes in a quadrant of the breast, usually the upper outer quadrant, are more characteristic of benign disorders; a slightly firmer thickening in one breast but not the other may be a sign of cancer.
Generated Simplification
During a breast exam, doctors may notice certain changes. Here’s what to know: - Sometimes, one area of the breast feels different from the rest. This is called a "dominant mass." It means there’s a lump or thickened area that stands out compared to the surrounding breast tissue. - If the breast feels generally thicker or firmer in one section, especially in the upper outer part, it’s often linked to non-cancerous (benign) conditions. These changes are usually not harmful. - However, if one breast has a firm or thickened area that isn’t present in the other breast, it could be a sign of something more serious, like breast cancer. If you notice any unusual changes in your breasts, it’s important to talk to your doctor. They can check to see if further tests are needed.
Reference Simplification
Such a lump may be cancer if it is a firm, distinctive thickening that appears in one breast but not the other. Usually, scattered lumpy changes in the breast, especially the upper outer region, are not cancerous and indicate fibrocystic changes.

Precision

0.255

0.222

Example 246 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 246
Source Text
More advanced breast cancers are characterized by one or more of the following: Fixation of the mass to the chest wall or to overlying skin; Satellite nodules or ulcers in the skin; Exaggeration of the usual skin markings resulting from skin edema caused by invasion of dermal lymphatic vessels (so-called peau d â orange).
Generated Simplification
Advanced breast cancer can show certain signs that make it different from earlier stages. These signs may include: - A lump in the breast that feels stuck to the chest wall or the skin above it. - Small lumps or sores on the skin near the main lump. - Changes in the skin of the breast, such as swelling that makes the skin look dimpled, like the surface of an orange. This happens because the cancer affects tiny lymph vessels in the skin. If you notice any of these changes, it’s important to talk to your doctor right away.
Reference Simplification
In more advanced stages, the lump usually adheres to the chest wall or the skin over it. If a breast contains cancer that adheres to the chest wall or skin, this maneuver may make the skin pucker or dimple or make one breast appear different from the other. In very advanced cancer, swollen bumps or festering sores may develop on the skin. Sometimes the skin over the lump is dimpled and leathery and looks like the skin of an orange (peau d â orange) except in color.

Two leading hypotheses suggest it may have jumped species from the disease scrapie in sheep, or that it evolved from a spontaneous form of `` mad cow disease '' that has been seen occasionally in cattle for many centuries.

Scientists think there are two main ideas about how this disease started: - It might have come from a disease in sheep called scrapie. - It could have developed on its own from a rare condition in cows called "mad cow disease," which has been around for hundreds of years. These are just theories, and more research is needed to understand it fully.

Example 248 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 248
Source Text
Myoclonus that occurs when patients are suddenly startled (startle myoclonus) may be an early symptom of Creutzfeldt-Jacob disease.
Generated Simplification
Sometimes, people with a condition called Creutzfeldt-Jacob disease (CJD) may experience sudden, jerky movements when they are startled. These movements are called "startle myoclonus." This can be one of the early signs of the disease. If you or someone you know is having these symptoms, it’s important to talk to a doctor. They can help figure out what’s causing the problem and what to do next.
Reference Simplification
In Creutzfeldt-Jacob disease (a rare degenerative brain disorder), myoclonus becomes more obvious when people are suddenly startled.

Example 253 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 253
Source Text
Because patients lack the motor responses (eg, withdrawal from painful stimuli) usually used to measure responsiveness, they may be mistakenly thought to be unconscious. Thus, all patients who can not move should have their comprehension tested by requesting eye blinking or vertical eye movements.
Generated Simplification
Sometimes, people who cannot move their bodies may seem unconscious because they don’t react to pain or other things in the usual way, like pulling away from something that hurts. To check if someone in this situation is aware and understands, doctors can ask them to blink their eyes or move their eyes up and down. This helps test their ability to respond without needing body movement.
Reference Simplification
Because locked-in syndrome can be mistaken for stupor or coma, doctors test people who do not move and appear unresponsive by asking them to open and close their eyes.

Example 254 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 254
Source Text
Bedside neurologic testing Neuropsychologic testing
Generated Simplification
Neurologic and neuropsychologic testing are ways doctors check how your brain and nervous system are working. Here's what they mean: **Neurologic Testing (Bedside Testing):** - This is a quick check done by your doctor, often in the office or at your bedside. - It looks at how well your brain, nerves, and muscles are working. - The doctor may ask you to do simple tasks, like following a light with your eyes, squeezing their hand, or walking in a straight line. - These tests help find problems with your brain, spinal cord, or nerves. **Neuropsychologic Testing:** - This is a more detailed test that looks at how your brain handles things like memory, attention, problem-solving, and language. - It’s usually done by a specialist and can take a few hours. - You might be asked to answer questions, solve puzzles, or remember words or pictures. - These tests can help find issues like memory loss, trouble focusing, or changes in thinking skills. If your doctor suggests these tests, it’s to better understand your symptoms and figure out the best care for you. If you have questions, talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
A doctor 's evaluation Standardized tests of brain function
BERTScore
Precision
-0.298
Recall
0.198
F1
-0.061

Example 257 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 257
Source Text
Cardiac complications include pericarditis (most commonly) and myocarditis.
Generated Simplification
Heart problems that can happen include: - **Pericarditis**: This is when the thin lining around the heart (called the pericardium) becomes swollen or irritated. It is the most common heart issue in this case. - **Myocarditis**: This is when the heart muscle itself becomes inflamed. If you have symptoms like chest pain, trouble breathing, or feel very tired, talk to your doctor right away.
Reference Simplification
People with lupus may have chest pain due to inflammation of the sac around the heart pericarditis). More serious but rare effects on the heart are inflammation of the walls of the coronary arteries (coronary artery vasculitis), which can lead to angina, and inflammation of the heart muscle (myocarditis), which can lead to heart failure.

Example 258 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 258
Source Text
Lifestyle changes have been shown to be effective in reducing (and in the case of diet, reversing) coronary disease: In people with coronary artery disease, aerobic exercise can reduce the risk of mortality.
Generated Simplification
Making changes to your daily habits can help improve heart health. In some cases, eating a healthier diet can even help reverse heart disease. Here’s what you should know: - If you have coronary artery disease (a condition where the blood vessels that supply your heart are narrowed or blocked), regular aerobic exercise can lower your risk of serious problems, including death. - Aerobic exercise includes activities like walking, jogging, swimming, or cycling. These exercises make your heart work harder and get stronger over time. Talk to your doctor about safe ways to start making these changes. They can help you create a plan that works for you.
Reference Simplification
Most people with coronary heart disease can feel healthy for years before they start experiencing symptoms.

In adults, the most common symptoms are: - Feeling very tired or drained (this is called lassitude). - Feeling weak or lacking strength. - Losing your appetite or not feeling like eating (this is called anorexia). Example 261 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 261
Source Text
The pain, lasting seconds to a few minutes, usually begins in the tonsillar region or at the base of the tongue and may radiate to the ipsilateral ear.
Generated Simplification
The pain usually starts in the area around the tonsils (the soft tissue at the back of your throat) or at the base of your tongue. It can last for a few seconds to a few minutes. Sometimes, the pain spreads to the ear on the same side as where it started.
Reference Simplification
The pain usually begins at the back of the tongue, back of the throat, or the area near the tonsils. Sometimes pain spreads to the ear or the area at the back of the jaw. The pain may last several seconds to a few minutes and usually affects only one side of the throat and tongue.

This process helps ensure that your DNA is copied accurately.

Example 265 | Profile: lower_education_level | Language: English | ID/Index: 265
Source Text
In developing countries, most cases are probably caused by tuberculosis, particularly in patients with impaired immune function due to undernutrition or human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection.
Generated Simplification
In many developing countries, tuberculosis (TB) is likely the main cause of these cases. This is especially true for people whose immune systems are weaker. A weak immune system can happen because of poor nutrition (not getting enough healthy food) or infections like HIV (a virus that makes it harder for the body to fight off illnesses).
